title: Adoption risks & next product-quality improvement for pilot expansion
summary: Docs changes improved quickstart completion, but adoption remains bottlenecked by setup/credential failures that lack a public recovery path and currently require support intervention. For pilot expansion, readiness gates emphasize making failed-install recovery self-serve and making recommendation evidence/citations auditable and clearly sourced.

claims.text: Quickstart completion improved from 44% to 58% after the docs copy refresh, with fewer exits at the first API call step.

The largest remaining drop-off occurs during local setup and credential configuration due to environment-specific errors; improvements benefited users who already had valid tokens more than brand-new users.

Support tickets are dominated by environment drift, stale CLI versions, missing environment variables, and credentials copied from the wrong workspace; users lack clear recovery steps after failed installs.
An internal support checklist resolves most setup issues, but its steps are not reflected in public docs; tickets often reopen when users restart the quickstart and reproduce the same failed state.
Pilot stakeholders flagged setup recovery as the most visible/brittle failure mode for demos; expansion should wait until failed-install guidance is documented and observable in the lab.

openQuestions: Which specific recovery steps from the internal support checklist should be promoted to a public, versioned “Setup Recovery” flow (and what safety/permissions caveats are needed)?
What product instrumentation will confirm recovery success (e.g., detecting stale CLI, missing env vars, workspace mismatch) without requiring support intervention?
What is the minimum viable “citation check” experience in the lab to satisfy the traceability prerequisite for recommendations (and how will it be tested during the pilot)?
recommendation: Prioritize a self-serve “Setup Recovery” improvement before adding more pilot teams: publish and productize a recovery checklist/flow that explicitly handles stale CLI installs, missing environment variables, credential/workspace mismatches, and repeated setup attempts—so a failed install has a documented path to a clean state without support pasting internal steps. This directly targets the main drop-off in setup/credentials and is an explicit pilot readiness gate blocking scheduling.
